commit.message:
Strip __Host-/__Secure- cookie prefixes when Secure flag is removed

When the proxy listens on plain HTTP, it strips the Secure flag from
Set-Cookie headers. However, cookies whose names begin with __Host- or
__Secure- also require the Secure attribute — without it browsers
silently reject the cookie, breaking any session that relies on them.

Fix: strip the prefix from the cookie name before stripping Secure.
This preserves the session value at the cost of the browser-enforced
security guarantee, which is acceptable in a local proxy/CTF context.

Tests: added three new sub-cases to TestRewriteSetCookiesUnit covering
  __Host- stripped (plain HTTP), __Secure- stripped (plain HTTP),
  __Host- kept (HTTPS proxy).

Also updated go-rewriting-reverse-proxy skill with new pitfalls:
SRI integrity stripping, __Host-/__Secure- cookies, Service-Worker-Allowed,
Vary: Accept-Encoding cleanup, protocol-relative URL rewriting.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Strip Link header integrity params and Service-Worker-Allowed header

Link header integrity:
  Link: </app.js>; rel=preload; integrity=sha256-xxx
  After proxy rewrites the resource, the precomputed hash is stale.
  Strip '; integrity=...' params from all Link header values so the
  browser doesn't block the preloaded (potentially rewritten) bytes.
  Added linkIntegrityRe and TestLinkHeaderIntegrityStripped.

Service-Worker-Allowed:
  A service worker registered via /__sd__/<host>/sw.js could set
  Service-Worker-Allowed: / and claim the entire proxy origin,
  intercepting all /__sd__/ routes. Strip this header to limit SW
  scope to the path it was served from.
  Added TestServiceWorkerAllowedStripped.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Enhance documentation for localhost certificate usage in Kestrun, including guidance for Chromium compatibility on Windows

Introduce support for development CA root + issuer-signed localhost leaves and update PowerShell surface/docs.

C#: Refactor CertificateManager to support issuer-signed certificates and CA creation: add DevelopmentCertificateOptions/Result records, NewDevelopmentCertificate, issuer material resolution, private-key extraction, validation, root trust (Windows store), EKU/key-usage resolution, signature algorithm selection, and helper methods for creating/trusting roots. SelfSigned generation now respects IsCertificateAuthority and optional IssuerCertificate.

PowerShell: Add New-KrDevelopmentCertificate cmdlet (and tests), update related certificate cmdlets for parameter/format consistency, export the new cmdlet in the module manifest, and tweak examples. Docs/tutorials/examples updated to recommend and explain the development root + localhost leaf workflow and export/import guidance.

Tests: Add PowerShell tests for new certificate flows and update C# certificate unit tests to cover new behavior.

* Set default key usage and update tests

Use explicitly provided KeyUsageFlags when present and refine ResolveKeyUsage defaults: CA certificates now include KeyCertSign|CrlSign, end-entity certs default to DigitalSignature with KeyEncipherment for RSA. Minor whitespace cleanup in several PowerShell certificate cmdlets. Update unit tests to assert the SKI extension type and compute AKI hex from raw ASN bytes (added GetAuthorityKeyIdentifierHex) instead of relying on formatted strings, making the comparison more robust.

* Improve cert private key handling and trust

Handle cases where ephemeral key loading produces non-exportable private keys and avoid exposing private keys when adding a root to Windows trust.

- Add GetPrivateKeyParameter overloads for RSA/ECDsa to fallback to DotNetUtilities when ExportPkcs8 fails.
- Add EnsureExportablePrivateKeySupport and SupportsPkcs8PrivateKeyExport to reload certificates without EphemeralKeySet when an exportable private key was requested but not produced.
- When trusting the development root, import a public-only certificate (no private key) into CurrentUser\Root to avoid exposing the private key.
- Surface clearer return text for development certificate methods/results indicating presence of the root in Windows CurrentUser Root after the operation.
- PowerShell: add ShouldProcess support (WhatIf/Confirm) to New-KrDevelopmentCertificate, update parameter docs, and guard root trust with ShouldProcess.
- Update examples and tests to reflect command name tweaks, ephemeral/exportable flag behavior, and added coverage for ShouldProcess/WhatIf semantics.

Also includes minor docs/text changes and small refactors (remove redundant Select, formatting).

* Fail TrustRoot on non-Windows; update docs/tests

PowerShell: Update New-KrCertificateRequest examples to return and reference the CsrResult object's CsrPem and PrivateKeyPem properties, and improve example descriptions.

PowerShell: Add an early terminating error in New-KrDevelopmentCertificate when -TrustRoot is used on non-Windows platforms, providing a clear message and ErrorRecord to prevent invoking the C# layer on unsupported platforms.

Tests: Update C# certificate tests to dispose created certificates with 'using var' to avoid resource leaks. Add a PowerShell Pester test that asserts New-KrDevelopmentCertificate fails early with a clear error when -TrustRoot is requested on non-Windows.

These changes clarify usage, enforce platform-specific behavior for trusting roots, and make tests more robust by ensuring proper disposal.

* Skip Windows-only test on non-Windows

Add -Skip:(!$IsWindows) to the New-KrDevelopmentCertificate It block in tests/PowerShell.Tests/Kestrun.Tests/New-KrDevelopmentCertificate.Tests.ps1 so the test is skipped on non-Windows platforms. This avoids failures for Windows-specific certificate root-trust behavior when running tests cross-platform.

* Add Join-KeyUsageFlags; refactor KeyUsage

Introduce a new private PowerShell helper Join-KeyUsageFlags to combine X509KeyUsageFlags into a single value and return $null if no flags are set. Replace duplicated key-usage bitwise-or logic in New-KrCertificateRequest.ps1 and New-KrSelfSignedCertificate.ps1 to call the new helper. Also tweak a C# exception message for clarity (include subject name) and fix minor comment/whitespace formatting in CertificateManager.cs.

Strip SRI integrity attributes from proxied HTML responses

When string replacement is active, modified script/stylesheet bytes no
longer match the SHA hash encoded in integrity="sha256-...", causing
the browser to block the resource with an SRI failure.

Strip all integrity= attributes from HTML responses unconditionally:
- even URL-only rewrites change document bytes so any proxied asset
  may differ from what the upstream hash was computed against
- crossorigin is left intact (controls credential mode, not just SRI)

Added sriIntegrityRe regex and TestSRIIntegrityStripped integration test.

feat: HTTPS support with automatic protocol discovery for RainBird v2 controllers (#45)

RainBird v2 firmware controllers use HTTPS with self-signed certificates
instead of plain HTTP. Without this change, the library fails to
communicate with any controller updated to the v2 firmware. See the
equivalent fix in the Python ecosystem:
[home-assistant/core#163915](https://github.com/home-assistant/core/pull/163915)
(pyrainbird v6.1.0).

**Changes Proposed in this Pull Request**

- **Protocol discovery on first request** (`RainBirdClient`): Probes
`https://{host}/stick` first; falls back to `http://{host}/stick` on
transport/TLS failure (e.g. `ECONNRESET`,
`ERR_SSL_WRONG_VERSION_NUMBER`). HTTP-level errors (403, 503) do not
trigger a fallback — HTTPS is correctly retained.
- **Self-signed cert acceptance**: Shared `Agent` with
`rejectUnauthorized: false` is used for HTTPS. This is a necessary
trade-off for local LAN devices with device-generated certificates;
documented in code comments.
- **Maintainable error classification**: Transport errors are matched
via a static `ReadonlySet` of error codes plus a `readonly` array of
message substrings, keeping the logic easy to extend.
- **Version bump**: `1.2.8` → `1.3.0`

```typescript
// First request probes HTTPS automatically — no API change required.
// Legacy HTTP controllers: HTTPS probe fails → falls back transparently.
// RainBird v2 controllers: HTTPS probe succeeds → all subsequent requests use HTTPS.
const service = new RainBirdService({ address, password, ... })
await service.init() // discovery happens here on getModelAndVersion()
```

**Version Impact**

- [ ] `patch` - Bug fixes and small improvements
- [x] `minor` - New features or enhancements
- [ ] `major` - Breaking changes

**Describe alternatives you've considered**

Exposing an explicit `useHttps` flag in `RainBirdService` options was
considered, but automatic discovery is strictly better: it's
backward-compatible, requires no user configuration change, and matches
the pyrainbird v6.1.0 approach.

**Additional context**

The discovery is done once per `RainBirdClient` instance and the result
is cached in `_url`. Since the `PQueue` enforces concurrency=1, there is
no race condition during discovery.

---------

commit.message:
Handle int overflow in rnn (#28003)

### Description

Fixes two overflow/underflow bugs in the CPU RNN kernel (`rnn.cc`):

- **`SafeInt` for GEMM M-dimension**: `seq_length * batch_size` was
computed as a raw `int64_t` multiply before `narrow<int>()`, meaning an
overflow would be UB before the check could fire. Replaced with
`SafeInt<int64_t>(seq_length) * batch_size` for a checked multiply.

- **`seq_length == 0` guard in `Assign_Y_h`**: For the forward
direction, `last_time_step = seq_length - 1` underflows to `-1` when
`seq_length == 0`, producing a negative `y_offset` and out-of-bounds
read. Added an early-exit that zero-fills Y_h for the direction and
returns. Also handles `sequence_lens[batch] == 0` (same underflow path),
zeroing the affected batch slot and skipping via `continue`.

### Motivation and Context

Silent UB from integer overflow/underflow in shape-derived index
arithmetic can corrupt memory or produce incorrect results without any
diagnostic signal. These cases are legal per the ONNX spec (empty
sequences, per-batch zero-length sequences) and must be handled
explicitly.

commit.message:
Fix sprite batch lighting bounds accumulation

Box3_Union was accumulating in->bounds (the first sprite) instead of
batch->bounds (the current sprite), so R_ActiveLights only considered
the first sprite's position for the entire batch. When that sprite was
far from an explosion, the lighting query found no lights, driving
diffuse to zero and making lit sprites invisible under additive
blending. Since particle order varies per frame, this caused flickering.

commit.message:
fix: prevent 304 bypass of body rewriting via conditional request caching

Two related bugs fixed:

1. Outbound conditional request headers not stripped
   The proxy was forwarding If-None-Match, If-Modified-Since, If-Match,
   If-Unmodified-Since, and If-Range to the upstream unchanged.  When the
   browser had a cached copy from a previous proxy run and resent these headers,
   the upstream could return 304 Not Modified.  ModifyResponse's noBody path
   skips all body rewriting on 304 responses, so the browser would then display
   its stale cached copy — which may contain unrewritten upstream hostnames.
   Fix: strip all five conditional headers in the Director before forwarding.

2. ETag + Last-Modified response headers not stripped
   By caching these validators the browser would eventually send conditional
   requests (see bug 1).  Strip both in headersStrip so the browser never has
   a validator to send for proxied resources.

Add TestConditionalRequestHeadersStripped and TestETagLastModifiedStripped.

behavior, and post-stream follow behavior while a thinking row trails

- threaded reveal keys through the assistant message text path so
resumed streaming entries can recover without replaying fresh streams
from blank

## Why this approach

The old renderer mixed transport timing, markdown parsing, and view
updates in one place, which made streaming feel visibly jumpy and
fragile around virtualization and async markdown handoff. This change
gives the stream a small timing core, keeps render optimizations local
to markdown, and lets follow behavior stay aligned with the actual
latest target.

## Validation

- targeted Bun tests for the reveal engine, reveal controller, and
thread-follow behavior
- targeted Vitest coverage for incremental streaming-tail parsing and
markdown streaming integration
- `bun run check` is still blocked by unrelated pre-existing TypeScript
errors in the modified-files / tool-call-edit area of the repo baseline

commit.message:
fix: skip body buffering for text/event-stream SSE responses

Server-Sent Events are long-lived streaming responses that must not pass
through io.ReadAll — doing so would block the client from receiving any
events until the stream closes (potentially never for persistent SSE).

Add an early-return in ModifyResponse when Content-Type is text/event-stream.
Header rewriting (host masking, CSP, etc.) still runs before the early exit,
so headers are correctly rewritten even for SSE responses.

FlushInterval=-1 on the ReverseProxy already handles progressive flushing;
this change ensures we don't inadvertently buffer the body first.

Add TestSSEPassthrough: spawns an upstream that writes one event then blocks
on a pipe; asserts the event is readable within 3 seconds (would time out if
the proxy was buffering the full stream).

commit.message:
⚡ Bolt: Optimize PythonJsonWorkerPool stderr failure reading (#217)

* ⚡ Bolt: Optimize PythonJsonWorkerPool stderr failure reading

The prior code used `ReadToEndAsync` to drain `stderr` when a Python worker process failed to return a valid response envelope. While this was wrapped in a cancellation token, `ReadToEndAsync` only cancels at internal read boundaries, meaning a process dumping megabytes of Python tracebacks would still cause large allocations and hang the application while the OS pipe buffer slowly flushed.

This change replaces `ReadToEndAsync` with a `ReadBoundedStderrAsync` method, which:
- Reads a maximum of 50 lines or 4096 characters using `ReadLineAsync` with strict `WaitAsync` boundaries.
- Silently drains the remainder of the pipe, allowing the OS pipe buffer to clear and ensuring the underlying subprocess can fully exit.
- Interpolates the previously unused `killSuffix` variable to accurately report when a kill operation was attempted before reading `stderr`.

This fix prevents hangs and OOM spikes when Python models crash.
